questions.prompt: Why send dynamic per-conversion values instead of static?

choices.text: tROAS needs accurate per-conversion values to bid properly. With static values ($100 per Purchase regardless of actual amount), the system can't distinguish a $20 order from a $2,000 order — same bid logic. With dynamic values (actual order subtotal per conversion), the system bids more aggressively on auctions predicted to drive high-value conversions and less on low-value ones. Per-impression value-bidding is the entire point of tROAS; static values forfeit it. E-commerce should always send dynamic values from the order subtotal.

questions.explanation: Dynamic values enable per-impression value-bidding. Static = forfeit tROAS's main benefit.

questions.prompt: Subscription business: optimizing for first-month payment as conversion value. Performance steady but unit economics suffer. Why?

choices.text: First-month payment under-represents customer value. A subscriber paying $50/month with average retention of 14 months is worth $700, not $50 — yet the system optimizes toward first-month revenue, finding users who pay once and may churn quickly. Move to LTV-based values: send predicted lifetime value at conversion time (built from a simple cohort model — average tenure × monthly payment × renewal probability). System now bids for high-LTV customers, not just first-payment users. Requires data-science work to build the LTV model but typically delivers significant improvement in unit economics within 60-90 days.

questions.explanation: Send LTV not first-month payment. Optimize for the real customer value.
